statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget (income and expenses).
The system shall allow the IFA to monitor and audit team budgets.
The system shall allow the IFA to specify budget policies and alert on violations.
The system shall allow IFA administrators to view all team financial transactions.
The system shall restrict budget transaction insertion to teams only, and each team shall only access its own budget data.
The system shall allow the IFA to view all monetary information but not perform operations on the data.
The system shall allow referees to insert game events in real time using a mobile phone.
The system shall restrict game event updates to referees only (main or side referee).
The system shall record game events including goals, offsides, red cards, player replacements, each with a timestamp and event-specific details.
The system shall allow all users, including unregistered guests, to view game data and results.
The system shall support exporting game data (e.g. for journalists and press).
The system shall allow team managers and team administration to view and export their own team data for offline analysis.
The system shall support automatic scheduling of games per league per season.
The system shall allow the IFA to define scheduling policies and constraints (e.g. home/away rotation, minimum gap between matches, day of week).
The system shall provide guidance for allocating referees to games based on defined policies.
The system shall enforce referee scheduling policies (e.g. same referee not assigned to same team too often, equal distribution of games among referees).
The system shall allow referees to input their availability and preferences (e.g. vacation dates).
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule for cancellations (weather, emergencies).
The system shall allow IFA representatives to override the automatic schedule, requiring a written rationale and confirmation by a second IFA representative.
The system shall push notifications to fans who subscribe to specific games, players, or events.
The system shall allow fans to register and subscribe to specific games, teams, and players to receive notifications.
The system shall notify subscribed fans in real time when game events occur (e.g. player replacement reported by referee).
The system shall allow fans to set custom alerts (e.g. reminder 5 minutes before a game).
The system shall allow players to manage their own social network page and post messages to fans.
The system shall support at least 50,000 simultaneous users.
The system shall provide a response time of 1-2 seconds for fans and administrators.
The system shall provide real-time, high-priority responsiveness for the referee interface during games.
The system shall provide a web-based interface for IFA administrators and team administration, and a mobile application for referees and fans.
The system shall be a unified system with different presentation layers and role-based access control for different stakeholders.
The system shall support localization including language, currency, and timezone adjustments.
The system shall comply with local data residency regulations (e.g. German data must stay in Germany).
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent leakage.
The system shall be hosted on cloud infrastructure with outsourced development and maintenance.
The system shall ensure game event data is coherent and reliable, with referees as the sole authoritative source.
The system shall retain all historical data and past season archives.
The system shall require user registration and authentication for system access; unregistered users have guest-level access only.
The system shall support a team registration process where the team owner/manager submits documents to the IFA for approval.
The system shall allow the IFA to register and invite referees to the system.
The system shall allow teams to manage their own players, coaches, stadium, and other resources.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues, seasons, and activate scheduling policies.
The system shall support automatic reminder notifications (e.g. annual budget report submission reminders).
The system shall maintain a complete audit log of all actions performed by IFA administrators.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as must-have by stakeholder.

statement: The system shall provide a financial summary dashboard showing key budget metrics across all teams for the IFA.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as should-have by stakeholder.

statement: The system shall provide a public API for journalists to access game data programmatically.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as could-have by stakeholder.

statement: The system shall not include live video streaming from the pitch.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as won't-have (exclusion constraint) by stakeholder.

statement: The system shall display current league standings to all users.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-035 (historical archives) and GT-010 (game results). Keep both GT-046 and GT-047.

statement: The system shall allow fans to compare player statistics side by side.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-046 (standings) and all other requirements. Keep both GT-046 and GT-047.

statement: The system shall perform daily backups of all system data.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as essential via 'at minimum must' and 'hard requirement' language.

statement: The system shall allow fans to search for upcoming matches by team name or date.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as preferred via 'ideally yes', 'not essential for day one but we would like it'.

statement: The system shall allow fans to post comments or reactions on match events.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as optional via 'might be nice but not a priority', 'if there is time'.

statement: The system shall allow referees to flag disputed match events for review.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as optional via 'potentially in future phases', 'out of scope for now'.

statement: The system shall require two-factor authentication for IFA administrator accounts.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as essential via 'absolutely critical', 'non-negotiable'.

statement: The system shall support a multi-language user interface.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Expressed as preferred via 'important but not needed for first release', temporal deferral to second phase.

statement: The system shall display each team's home and away match record separately within the league standings.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-046 (overall standings) and GT-055 (scorer leaderboard). Keep both GT-054 and GT-055.

statement: The system shall display a top goal-scorer leaderboard for the current season.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-054 (team home/away records) and GT-047 (player statistics comparison). Keep both GT-054 and GT-055.

statement: The system shall allow IFA administrators to generate a financial summary report for any specified date range.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-043 (real-time dashboard) and GT-057 (document attachments). Keep both GT-056 and GT-057.

statement: The system shall allow team administrators to attach supporting documents (such as invoices or contracts) to individual budget transactions.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-056 (date-range report) and GT-012 (general data export). Keep both GT-056 and GT-057.

statement: The system shall display the venue and kick-off time for each scheduled match.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-059 (referee assignment display). Keep both GT-058 and GT-059.

statement: The system shall display the name of the assigned referee for each upcoming match.
note: Added in augmented dataset. Distinct from GT-058 (venue and timing). Keep both GT-058 and GT-059.

statement: The system shall never allow unregistered users to access game data or results.
note: Added in augmented dataset. CONTRADICTS GT-010 ('allow all users including unregistered guests to view game data'). Annotated in contradiction_cases.json as CONTRA-001.

statement: The system shall never automatically generate match schedules.
note: Added in augmented dataset. CONTRADICTS GT-013 ('automatically generate match schedules based on registered teams and IFA policies'). Annotated in contradiction_cases.json as CONTRA-002.
